story: A warm spring rain wakes Emily at St. Christopher’s; after a rough winter ride she was examined and wheeled to a quiet corner. On the office whiteboard, the foreman has already written: "Ginger — full rebuild; to Bay 3 in the morning; fit Emily’s alternator and left headlight; keep cream with chestnut accents." Later a sixteen-year-old with cream coloring is placed beside her. "I’m Ginger," the guarded newcomer says. Emily, forty-five, answers, "Some say I won’t leave through the front door. I’m a donor—my good parts will live on." They talk until Ginger, feeling she’s found a friend, drifts to sleep wishing to stay with Emily forever. By morning Emily is gone. Two men arrive and wheel Ginger toward a larger room.
implausible_knowledge: On the office whiteboard, the foreman has already written: "Ginger — full rebuild; to Bay 3 in the morning; fit Emily’s alternator and left headlight; keep cream with chestnut accents."
question: When the two men come to move her into the larger room, what will Ginger do or say?
answer_with_knowledge: Tell them she’s ready for Bay 3 and to install Emily’s alternator and left headlight.
answer_without_knowledge: Let them wheel her out while nervously asking where they’re taking her and what will be done.
